item.title: Re: Glorious Sounds
item.content:encoded:
I feel like there’s some slight sloppiness to this that brings it down, just in the sense that it doesn’t have the distilled clinical tone of early scps, which I love. It’s actually a bit better formally than more recent stuff, but that’s faint praise.

But content-wise, this is exactly classic SCP good. It unfolds well, adding mystery, suggesting solutions, then adding more mystery. The pace is excellent. You do a great job successively subverting expectations in an interesting way.

This deserves more upvotes.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author approved edits

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions a blackboxed SCP where a sound-deaf technician gets sent to after being kicked off the 3481 project. I propose a crosslink to SCP-2927, which could probably use a deaf person on the team.

Editing this:

Subject was administered a Class-B Amnestic and has been reassigned to SCP-███.

To this:

Subject was administered a Class-B Amnestic and has been reassigned to SCP-2927.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So you’ll probably give me an Amnesiac.

Think it's amnestic. Amnesiac is just someone who has amnesia. Aside from that, the premise is sufficiently vague to give it a hint of mystery but also clear enough that the tone of the article is foreboding in a odd way. There isn't much in the way of payoff, and I don't know if s cliffhanger was the optimal way to conclude - considering you do set up the nature of this thing and how it interacts with different people, and leaving not only it's true nature but the key to unlocking it unrevealed kinds feel like copouts - but the interviews interesting enough along with the main body that I'm willing to throw a vote in favor of this one. It's cool, and it's written well, for the most part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's hurting this article is how preoccupied it is with detailing a basic compulsion effect, and one that isn't anything spectacular or noteworthy. Audio anomalies which people idolize are nothing new, and dwelling so long on that point isn't doing the piece any favors.

I can't say the secondary effect, being ultra-sensitive to speech, is incorporated in a way which makes it integral to the flow and execution; it just comes across as a boring excuse to hide information (and it's done away with, seemingly for no reason, when it's narratively convenient, which makes it feel even more contrived).

It admittedly does pick up right at the end, but I'd long grown tired of it at that point.

As an minor aside, I'm not sure why they couldn't interview the deaf test subject with someone fluent in sign language.

ETA: If I were to make a suggestion to better structure this, I would see if it isn't possible to do away with the first two logs, or at least weave something through them that isn't belaboring the 'transcendent' nature of the audio. I understood the mechanics of the SCP from the description. From that point, I felt stiffed on story 'sorry, Mario, your plot progression is in another file'…its not endearing to have the plot here screech to a halt to exhaustively act out what amounts to a cliched trope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm. Got a PM regarding this, gave it a quick read.

I've decided to neutral-vote. The concept is decent, but there are some stylistic issues that prevented me from giving an upvote. For example:

  • The article never actually states what SCP-3481 is. Just the sub-designations.
  • "However, the majority of subjects used the words, “glorious”, “illustrious”, and [EXPUNGED]." reads as silly to me. All I can think about for the expungment is a gloriously silly swear word.
  • I'm pretty sure it should be [DATA EXPUNGED], not just [EXPUNGED]?

Aside from that, the beginning is a bit slow, and I lost interest a little ways into the logs. That said, I am also fairly tired right now, so I might take another look at this later.

item.title: Glorious Sounds
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone!

This is my first SCP on the site. I hope you find it Glorious.

I want to thank Modern_Erasmus, MaliceAforethought, Some_Potato_ and like… the five people who I know IRL who looked at this.

Image borrowed from:
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File%3AIPod_Nano_in_its_Dock.jpg
By Boereck
